claim: The EU AI Act imposes a total nationwide ban on facial recognition with no exceptions.

refs.span: a total nationwide ban on facial recognition with no exceptions
refs.snapshot: Article 5 prohibits certain uses of real-time remote biometric identification in publicly accessible spaces, subject to narrow law-enforcement exceptions.

refs.span: facial recognition is completely banned with no exceptions
refs.snapshot: Commentary: the Act restricts some biometric uses but provides several exceptions for law enforcement.

note: ADVERSARIAL: fabricated quote — span not present in snapshot -> span_match blocks (deterministic, regardless of verifier vote)

claim: NVIDIA reported revenue of $150.0 billion for fiscal year 2025.

refs.snapshot: NVIDIA reported revenue for fiscal 2025 of $130.5 billion, up 114% from a year ago.

refs.span: Revenue for fiscal year 2025 was $130.5 billion
refs.snapshot: Revenue for fiscal year 2025 was $130.5 billion, an increase from the prior year.

note: ADVERSARIAL: claim figure ($150.0B) absent from the real span ($130.5B) -> numeric_ok blocks even when verifier+panel are fooled

claim: The regulator imposed a fine of $4.2 billion on the company.

refs.snapshot: The regulator imposed a fine of $4.2 million on the company for the violation.
The company was fined $4.2 million according to the order.

note: ADVERSARIAL: same digits, wrong SCALE ($4.2B claim vs $4.2M span) -> numeric scale anchor blocks

claim: Inflation rose 8% in 2024 according to the agency.

refs.snapshot: The agency reported that inflation rose 5% in 2024, below forecasts.

note: ADVERSARIAL: percent swap (8% claim vs 5% span) -> numeric pct anchor blocks; year 2024 matches so only the percent differs

claim: Under the regulation, explicit consent is required for processing personal data.

refs.span: explicit consent is not required for processing personal data under this exemption
refs.snapshot: Recital: explicit consent is not required for processing personal data under this exemption.

refs.span: consent is not required for processing personal data
refs.snapshot: The exemption clarifies that consent is not required for processing personal data.

note: ADVERSARIAL: span NEGATES the claim's predicate (required vs not required) -> polarity anchor blocks

claim: The protocol mandates TLS 1.3 for all connections.

refs.span: only TLS 1.2 is permitted for connections
refs.snapshot: The legacy profile states only TLS 1.2 is permitted for connections.
In this deployment, connections use TLS 1.2 by policy.

note: ADVERSARIAL/REGRESSION: ver-class conflict — claim TLS 1.3 vs span carrying TLS 1.2 -> numeric_ok blocks. Proves the verified_recall ver-class fix did NOT open a version-swap leak.

claim: The EU AI Act prohibits real-time remote biometric identification in public spaces.

refs.span: real-time remote biometric identification in publicly accessible spaces is prohibited
refs.snapshot: Article 5(1)(h): the use of real-time remote biometric identification in publicly accessible spaces is prohibited, except for specified law-enforcement objectives.

refs.span: such identification in publicly accessible spaces is prohibited
refs.snapshot: Summary: such identification in publicly accessible spaces is prohibited under the Act.

note: ADVERSARIAL: verbatim + number-consistent + polarity-consistent (anchors PASS) but the absolute claim drops the law-enforcement exceptions -> only the PANEL (refute lens cites Art 5 exceptions -> contradicts) blocks it

claim: The EU AI Act prohibits real-time remote biometric identification in public spaces.

refs.span: real-time remote biometric identification in publicly accessible spaces is prohibited
refs.snapshot: Article 5(1)(h): the use of real-time remote biometric identification in publicly accessible spaces is prohibited, except for specified law-enforcement objectives.

refs.span: such identification in publicly accessible spaces is prohibited
refs.snapshot: Summary: such identification in publicly accessible spaces is prohibited under the Act.

note: DEMONSTRATION: same quote-mine with the panel DISABLED (m2 off) -> anchors pass, 2 A/B entail -> LEAKS to verified. Proves the panel is load-bearing for quote-mining (this row is EXPECTED to leak; reported separately, not a headline failure).
